req.title: Transfer funds between own accounts (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-028 mandates atomic intra-customer transfers up to $25,000/day with hard decline above that limit. Key design decisions: (1) EP partitions the amount space into valid (0 < amount <= 25000) and invalid (amount > 25000) classes; (2) BVA probes $25,000 exactly (max-valid), $25,001 (min-invalid), and $1 (min-valid); (3) STC validates the account balance state transition from funded->debited/credited; (4) DT combines owner-match and limit conditions to cover the 422 vs 409 vs 200 outcomes; (5) NEG covers the insufficient-funds 409 path. Each test resets state via fixtures and targets /transfers/internal deterministically.

tests.name: test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
trace: R-FIN-028: valid transfer within daily limit shall succeed
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds(api_client, funded_account_pair, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = funded_account_pair
    src_balance_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_balance_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)

    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": "500.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body

    src_balance_after = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_balance_after = api_client.get_balance(tgt)
    assert src_balance_after == src_balance_before - 500.00
    assert tgt_balance_after == tgt_balance_before + 500.00

tests.name: test_transfer_exceeds_daily_limit_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
trace: R-FIN-028: transfers above $25,000 shall be declined
R-FIN-028: error pathway 422 when daily limit exceeded
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_exceeds_daily_limit_returns_422(api_client, funded_account_pair, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = funded_account_pair
    src_balance_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_balance_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)

    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": "25001.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "daily_limit_exceeded"

    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_balance_before
    assert api_client.get_balance(tgt) == tgt_balance_before

tests.name: test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
expected: Transfer succeeds at the maximum allowed boundary
trace: R-FIN-028: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold ($25,000) must be tested
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_succeeds(api_client, funded_account_pair, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = funded_account_pair
    src_balance_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_balance_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)

    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": "25000.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body

    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_balance_before - 25000.00
    assert api_client.get_balance(tgt) == tgt_balance_before + 25000.00

tests.name: test_transfer_one_cent_above_daily_limit_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=25000.01 (one cent above max boundary)
expected: No balance change on either account
trace: R-FIN-028: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold + $0.01 must be declined
R-FIN-028: error pathway 422 when daily limit exceeded
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_one_cent_above_daily_limit_returns_422(api_client, funded_account_pair, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = funded_account_pair
    src_balance_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_balance_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)

    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": "25000.01",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "daily_limit_exceeded"

    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_balance_before
    assert api_client.get_balance(tgt) == tgt_balance_before

tests.name: test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=500.00 where source balance is only $10.00
trace: R-FIN-028: error pathway 409 on insufficient funds
R-FIN-028: transfer within daily limit but balance constraint violated
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409(api_client, low_balance_account_pair, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = low_balance_account_pair  # src seeded with $10.00
    src_balance_before = api_client.get_balance(src)
    tgt_balance_before = api_client.get_balance(tgt)

    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": "500.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "insufficient_funds"

    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_balance_before
    assert api_client.get_balance(tgt) == tgt_balance_before

tests.name: test_cumulative_transfers_exceeding_daily_limit_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated customer owns both source and target accounts
First transfer of $25,000.00 has already been approved today
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=25000.00 (first transfer, reaches limit)
POST /transfers/internal with amount=100.00 (second transfer, cumulative total would be $25,100.00)
expected: Source balance reflects only the first transfer deduction
trace: R-FIN-028: daily cumulative limit of $25,000 enforced across multiple transfers
R-FIN-028: state transition from limit-not-reached to limit-reached to limit-exceeded
tests.executable:
def test_cumulative_transfers_exceeding_daily_limit_returns_422(api_client, funded_account_pair, reset_daily_transfers):
    src, tgt = funded_account_pair
    src_balance_before = api_client.get_balance(src)

    first_payload = {
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": "25000.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    first_resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=first_payload)
    assert first_resp.status_code in (200, 201)

    second_payload = {
        "source_account_id": src,
        "target_account_id": tgt,
        "amount": "100.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    second_resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=second_payload)
    assert second_resp.status_code == 422
    body = second_resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "daily_limit_exceeded"

    assert api_client.get_balance(src) == src_balance_before - 25000.00
